records.ety: [F., fr. L. profanus, properly, before the temple, i. e., without the temple, unholy; pro before + fanum temple. See 1st Fane.]

defs: Not sacred or holy; not possessing peculiar sanctity; unconsecrated; hence, relating to matters other than sacred; secular; -- opposed to sacred, religious, or inspired; as, a profane place.
quotes: The profane wreath was suspended before the shrine.
Nothing is profane that serveth to holy things.

defs: Treating sacred things with contempt, disrespect, irreverence, or undue familiarity; irreverent; impious.
Irreverent in language; taking the name of God in vain; given to swearing; blasphemous; as, a profane person, word, oath, or tongue.

records.ety: [L. profanare: cf. F. profaner. See Profane, a.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Profaned (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Profaning.]

defs: To violate, as anything sacred; to treat with abuse, irreverence, obloquy, or contempt; to desecrate; to pollute; as, to profane the name of God; to profane the Scriptures, or the ordinance of God.
quotes: The priests in the temple profane the sabbath.

defs: To put to a wrong or unworthy use; to make a base employment of; to debase; to abuse; to defile.
quotes: So idly to profane the precious time.
